headline: Global CEO & Founder
summary:
Damian Cox is the Global CEO and Founding Partner of Wildstone, a company he established in 2010. He’s a serial entrepreneur with a deep background in outdoor advertising. His career kicked off in 1999 with BlowUp Media, but he quickly struck out on his own, founding E.K. Straas Ltd. in 2001, which he sold to Clear Channel UK. In 2004, he launched Ocean Outdoor, turning it into a leader in premium digital outdoor advertising before selling it to Smedvig Capital in 2008 and offloading his remaining stake in 2012 when Lloyds Development Capital took over.
With Wildstone, Damian initially focused on helping property owners—both public and private—maximize land value through outdoor advertising, emphasizing innovative design. The company has since built a reputation for creating many of the UK’s most iconic advertising sites. In 2015, he shifted Wildstone’s strategy toward acquiring and digitizing outdoor advertising assets, partnering with France-based financial backers to scale it into Europe’s largest aggregator of such infrastructure. Today, Wildstone boasts over 5,300 assets across the UK, Ireland, the Netherlands, Germany, and Spain, with a strong push toward converting traditional billboards into sleek, high-quality digital screens.
Damian's approach blends a passion for design with a sharp business sense, aiming to make billboards both functional and visually striking. His track record shows a knack for spotting market gaps, building businesses, and cashing out at the right moment—Wildstone being his latest and most expansive venture yet

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description:
Damian Cox founded Wildstone in 2010 to provide property owners, public and private, with a resource to assist with the capitalisation of land that could be used for outdoor advertising. We focused on design and visionary implementation and as a result, we have been responsible for developing the large majority of high profile and iconic outdoor sites in the UK.

We expanded in 2015, focusing on the purchase of leasehold and freehold outdoor advertising sites and are on route to become the largest aggregation fund of media assets across Europe.
